qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via afterAllFilesWrite hook execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection via the afterAllFilesWrite hook configuration mechanism, which is a different vulnerability than the advisory-described code injection via unsanitized x-enum-descriptions. The advisory specifically identifies the vulnerable code as the jsStringEscape function in packages/core/src/utils/string.ts failing to sanitize * and / characters in x-enum-descriptions, allowing JavaScript comment block escape via */ sequences. The hook-command-injection finding addresses a separate attack vector through the hooks configuration execution path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory specifically describes code injection in Orval via unsanitized x-enum-descriptions in OpenAPI specs, where the jsStringEscape function in packages/core/src/utils/string.ts fails to sanitize * and / characters, allowing attackers to break out of JavaScript comment blocks using */ sequences. Finding 'hook-command-injection' addresses a different Orval vulnerability (command execution via hook configuration mechanism).
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gch2-phqh-fg9q specifically identifies vulnerable code at packages/core/src/utils/string.ts:227 in the jsStringEscape function
Advisory describes the attack vector as unsanitized x-enum-descriptions in OpenAPI specs allowing JavaScript comment escape via */ sequences
Finding 'hook-command-injection' targets the afterAllFilesWrite hook execution mechanism at packages/orval/src/utils/execute-hook.ts:38-40, which is a different code location and attack surface
